https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-4-cryptobeholder-vyn6f?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BmWgHfai9RGSYAcuYsJauuA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3MeLEXM7jlQYg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpzpcprq~jo-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpzpcprq-jo
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Extreme fear dominates as Bitcoin crashes to a four-month low, liquidating $1.6 billion in longs. Record ETF outflows and a symbolic MicroStrategy sale have shifted sentiment toward a structural de-risking phase. 🚨Bitcoin Crashes to $61,000 Amidst Massive Liquidation Wave Bitcoin plummeted to a four-month low of $61,300 today, triggering over $1.61 billion in market-wide liquidations. The sharp decline was fueled by record institutional outflows and heightened geopolitical tensions, wiping out over 290,000 leveraged traders. Read full story 📉Spot Bitcoin ETFs Record Historic 12-Day Outflow Streak U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs have shed a cumulative $3.58 billion over the last 12 trading days, the longest redemption streak since their 2024 launch. BlackRock’s IBIT saw its second-largest single-day withdrawal, signaling a major cooling of institutional appetite. See details 💰MicroStrategy Breaks "Never Sell" Stance with Symbolic Disposal MicroStrategy disclosed the sale of 32 Bitcoin to fund preferred stock distributions, its first disposal since 2022. While operationally immaterial, the move has rattled investor confidence in the firm’s long-standing permanent treasury strategy. Read more 🏛️Robinhood Enters Canada via $180M WonderFi Acquisition Robinhood Markets has officially closed its acquisition of Canadian digital asset firm WonderFi, bringing 300,000 new customers into its ecosystem. The deal provides Robinhood with immediate regulatory standing and infrastructure to scale its crypto services across North America. See full announcement 📉Ethereum Slumps to 14-Month Low as ETF Optimism Fades Ethereum (ETH) dropped below the critical $1,900 level to touch a 14-month low of $1,738 during today's market rout. Analysts cite a "rally that wasn't," as delays in ETF filings and broader macro headwinds weigh heavily on the second-largest asset. Read full story 🔄Institutional Rotation: Capital Shifts from Crypto to AI Equities Market data reveals a significant rotation of capital out of crypto risk assets and into AI-focused tech stocks. This strategic shift is being driven by sticky inflation data and a narrowing premium on crypto-treasury corporate strategies. See market analysis 📈Ripple (XRP) Institutional Inflows Defy Broader Market Bleed Despite the global market crash, XRP-based investment product

74053.subject: Andrew Yang Buys Helium Mobile
74053.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

Gm, and happy Thursday! 

Crypto slipped lower as equities closed Wednesday’s session in the red and futures traded lower overnight. ETFs continue to show outflows while STRC trades off par, adding pressure to the persistent bear. 

Meanwhile, Andrew Yang’s Noble Mobile acquires Helium Mobile. What does Noble see that others might have missed?

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

BTC traded back down to $61.3K following five straight days of negative price action. This marks the lowest price in 100 days as the rally of Q2 has largely retraced and proved to be counter-trend. While BTC is knocking on the lows, equities remain ~1.3% off of their all-time highs. Notably, within crypto, BTC is leading to the downside. Over the past week, the majority of crypto indices have outperformed BTC, raising the question of whether breadth and dispersion shows internal health, or if correlations move back towards 1 for the next move lower in alts. Perps remain the top performing sector on the week, as LIT, HYPE, and MYX trade strong.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/15ad09b0-ac5f-4943-83df-56ebe0f8fa9b/image.png?t=1780579878)
Caption: 

ETF flows haven’t found their floor, either. For BTC and ETH, daily net flows remained stubbornly negative throughout the second half of May, with the selling continuing into June. Meanwhile, SOL and HYPE ETFs show modest inflows.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1c52425c-2ad2-4cfd-a669-dd0530763693/image.png?t=1780579878)
Caption: 

With persistent negative returns on BTC, Saylor is under pressure as STRC closed Wednesday at $95, $5 off par. Strategy’s moves over the past week, using cash to buy back convertible notes and selling 32 BTC, may have led to some unintended consequences. To begin, the viability of STRC embeds the assumption that the BTC purchased with proceeds from STRC issuance will appreciate in excess of the dividend obligation, net of price impact, which has certainly not been the case since issuance began amid BTC’s bear market. Similarly, a reduced cash pile from the convertible repurchase lowers Strategy’s cash runway for dividend payments, raising the concern that MSTR would need to sell more BTC to cover this. The market smells this out, with BTC moving risk-off in advance of these flows. Effectively, STRC trading off par is like credit spreads widening, with

Fear and Greed sits at 

 Extreme Fear
and BTC is near
$62K
this morning, down from $70,000 earlier this week. BTC found a
partial floor overnight after the US Treasury Secretary told the
Senate the government is actively building a strategic Bitcoin
reserve.

The week's selling has been severe. But the policy signals
coming out of Washington are the most constructive in years. A
rare physical Bitcoin just moved after fifteen years. And Zcash
quietly fixed a critical bug while its price kept climbing.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Citi Says Bitcoin's Demand Problem Is Bigger Than One Sale

Citi says Bitcoin's decline is driven by weak investor
demand, not Strategy's small Bitcoin sale. ETF flows explain
roughly 45% of weekly Bitcoin return variation, the bank
estimates.

The current outflow streak is the longest since spot Bitcoin
ETFs launched in 2024. Without CLARITY Act progress, Citi warns
sentiment may stay muted.

What does Citi say could bring fresh demand back into Bitcoin?

74042.subject: Team1 Applications Are Open: Build The Future Of Avalanche
74042.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/team1-applications-are-open-build

Team1 has spent the past few years doing the ground-level work of making Avalanche more accessible. 450+ members across 40 countries, 6 official chapters, 600+ community events, 75,000+ Luma subscribers growing 15% month over month, and collaborations with 130 universities. Six country chapters are live, with at least 6 more set to launch before the end of 2026.
Now, Team1 is looking for the next group to grow the Avalanche ecosystem.
Applications opened May 27th and close June 16th.
Why This Moment
Avalanche’s ecosystem is moving fast, and the gaps where community support makes a real difference are getting harder to ignore. New L1s are launching, developer tooling is maturing, and the user base is expanding into regions that are just starting to find their footing. Team1 is growing its core global team and rolling out 12+ new regional chapters this year.
The people who join now will shape what that expansion looks like.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
What Team1 Actually Does
Team1 is a global community built around growing the Avalanche ecosystem through education, events, content, and direct support for builders and newcomers. Members work in their local regions and across time zones to help people understand Avalanche, establish a presence, and join the ecosystem.
What that looks like varies. Some members run hackathons or university workshops. Others write guides, produce content, or help onboard developers and founders. Some show up through Discord, X, regional calls, or by connecting builders with the right people at the right time. Team1 supports all of it: event funding pathways, content bounty opportunities, creative support, and direct connections across the ecosystem.
It’s real work that impacts the ecosystem.
The Kind of Person Team1 Is Looking For
Team1 is built for people who show up consistently in ways that fit their schedule and their strengths. You don’t need to be a full-time Web3 professional or someone who can host events every week. Whether you are a founder, event host, developer, graphic designer, videographer, or business development professional, we are looking for people who bring their unique skills to the table.
Integrity matters most. Members represent the community, and that carries weight. Beyond that, Team1 looks for people who take initiative without being asked, who are willing to try something new even when it doesn’t land perfectly, and who want to help others navigate the ecosystem rather than just building their own profile.
Collaboration is built into how Team1 operates. Members work across regions and countries, share ideas, and support each other’s local efforts. If you find satisfaction in helping something or someone grow, you’ll fit in here.
What You Get Out of It
Team1 gives you the infrastructure
